title: The Effect of Prescription Drug Monitoring Programs on Opioid Utilization in Medicare

construct_vars: df$eventhalfyear[state_ch %in% c('New York','Tennessee')] <- df$eventhalfyear[state_ch %in% c('New York','Tennessee')] - 2
df <- as.data.frame(dplyr::group_by(df, ssastate) |> dplyr::mutate(evermustaccess = max(mustaccess, na.rm=TRUE)))

notes: TWO SEPARATE EVENT STUDY PDFs — event_study_script.R produces: (a) event_study_com_binning.pdf (window -4 to 2, F4 bins eventhalfyear<=-4) and (b) event_study_sem_binning.pdf (window -9 to 2, separate F9-F2 dummies). CRITICAL FIX: event dummies must be 0 for never-treated states (evermustaccess==0) — otherwise never-treated states get L0=1 at halfyear=105 and dilute treatment effect by ~50%. Fix: eventhalfyear_t = ifelse(evermustaccess==1, eventhalfyear, NA). ES sample excludes NV/LA/NY/TN (es_sample_filter). Stata halfyear integers: th2012h2=105, th2012h1=104, th2011h1=102. gvar_CS: OK=102, DE/OH=104, KY/NM/WV=105, never-treated=0. MATCHES: TWFE com binning EXACT (F4=0.00067, F3=-0.00171, F2=-0.00130, L0=-0.00353); BJS EXACT (tau0=-0.00356, tau1=-0.00179, tau2=-0.00099). APPROXIMATIONS: TWFE sem L2 slightly off (R=-0.00127 vs Stata=-0.00100). SA from sunab(gvar_SA=Inf for NT). BJS via did_imputation v0.5.0 with yname/gname/tname/idname args — R package cannot estimate pre-period BJS (negative horizons silently ignored). Point estimate TWFE -0.00187 matches Stata exactly. CS-NT FIX (Round 3): Stata uses csdid2 with long2 aggregation (averages over event-time periods = dynamic). R aggte(type='simple') gave -0.00155 (14.9% gap). aggte(type='dynamic') gives -0.001888 (3.7% gap, SE=0.000859 vs Stata SE~0.00123). results.csv updated to use type='dynamic' value.
